dateline: Johnson Space Center, Houston / Kennedy Space Center — 2017–Present
heroHeadline: NASA Formally Shifts Focus to Artemis III — Crewed Lunar Landing Milestones Outlined Post-Artemis II
heroSubtitle: Apr 13: NASA formally pivots programmatic focus to Artemis III following the successful Artemis II lunar flyby. Agency outlines near-term milestones for crewed lunar landing missions. Orion capsule en route to KSC for its 30-day heat shield inspection — results will directly inform Artemis IV readiness (2028 target).
footerNote: Data sourced from NASA.gov, OIG Reports, SpaceNews, Ars Technica, Congressional appropriations. Tier 1: NASA/ESA/Official. Tier 2: SpaceNews/Ars Technica/Reuters. Tier 3: Planetary Society/AIAA.
